figures.caption: Fig. 5.2. Effect of the block size |B| on the convergence of Algorithm 3. The figure reports the suboptimality gap fj − f∗ averaged over 10 independent runs, versus oracle calls for for “stochastic” EASIeST with block sizes |B| ∈ {n, 0.9n, 0.8n, 0.7n, 0.6n, 0.5n, 0.4n, 0.3n, 0.2n, (1− α)n}. In all experiments, the mini-batch size is fixed at Nα, α = 0.95.

Fig. 5.1. Comparison of the average suboptimality gap fj − f⋆, averaged over 10 independent runs, versus oracle calls for “stochastic” EASIeST and the baseline method under different batch sizes, ranging from 0.5Nα to Nα, α = 0.95.
